query: Mexico registro lineas celulares prorroga junio 2026 CRT Castañeda latest developments
answer:
The first agent successfully retrieved substantial information. Let me compile the complete research report.

---

## Mexico Cell Phone Line Registration: Latest Developments (as of June 2, 2026)

### Executive Summary

Mexico's mandatory cell phone line registration program — administered by the **Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones (CRT)** — has a hard deadline of **June 30, 2026**. Despite only ~51.6 million of an estimated 144–158 million lines being registered as of late May 2026 (~33%), CRT officials — including **Ricardo Castañeda Álvarez**, Director General de Política Regulatoria — have categorically confirmed **there will be no prórroga (extension)**. Unregistered lines face suspension starting July 1, 2026.

---

### 1. Current Registration Status

- **Program launch**: January 9, 2026
- **Deadline**: June 30, 2026
- **Mechanism**: Users must link their SIM/phone line to their **CURP** (Clave Única de Registro de Población) and official ID
- **No biometric data required** (unlike the original PANAUT struck down by the SCJN in 2022)
- **Data storage**: Decentralized — each carrier holds its own user data; no centralized government database
- **Access by authorities**: Only through judicial orders

**Registration Progress (cumulative):**

| Date | Lines Registered | % of Total |
|------|-----------------|------------|
| Early April 2026 | ~27 million | ~17% |
| April 19, 2026 | ~30.2 million | ~19.1% |
| Mid-May 2026 | ~48–49.5 million | ~31% |
| May 27, 2026 | ~51.6 million | ~33% |

**By carrier (mid-May 2026):**
- AT&T: ~29% registered
- Bait (Grupo Walmart): ~28%
- Telcel (América Móvil): ~19%
- Movistar (Telefónica): ~16%

At the current pace (~1.5M lines/day needed), full compliance by June 30 appears **extremely unlikely**.

---

### 2. No Prórroga — CRT's Position

The CRT has been emphatic and consistent: **no extension will be granted.**

- **April 21, 2026**: Comisionada Presidenta **Norma Solano** confirmed during the "Registra tu línea" campaign launch that the June 30 deadline is firm, with no possibility of extension.
- **May 28, 2026**: **Ricardo Castañeda Álvarez** (CRT Director General de Política Regulatoria) stated on Radio Fórmula (with Ciro Gómez Leyva): *"No hay discusión alguna"* — there is absolutely no discussion about an extension. He emphasized that only Congress (Congreso de la Unión) has the authority to modify the legally established deadline.
- Multiple outlets (La Silla Rota, La Jornada, El Informador, Infobae, El Siglo de Torreón, Proyecto Puente) all confirmed this message around May 28–29, 2026.

**Industry's earlier extension request (January 2026):** Telecom companies, particularly MVNOs, formally petitioned the CRT for at least a one-month delay, citing technical problems and registration costs ($3.45 pesos + IVA per attempt, with frequent failures). The CRT rejected this.

---

### 3. Ricardo Castañeda Álvarez's Role

Castañeda is the **Director General de Política Regulatoria** of the CRT — a senior operational official (not a commissioner). He has been the **primary public spokesperson** for the registration program's implementation, responsible for:
- Providing registration progress statistics to media
- Confirming the no-prórroga position
- Explaining consequences for non-compliance
- Detailing that only Congress can modify the deadline
- Appearing in interviews with major outlets including Radio Fórmula and Milenio

---

### 4. Consequences for Non-Registration (After June 30)

- Lines will be **suspended** (not permanently canceled) starting **July 1, 2026**
- Suspended lines **cannot**: make calls, send SMS, or use mobile data
- Suspended lines **can still**: call 911 and receive civil protection alerts
- Users can **reactivate at any time** by completing registration — they do not lose their number or balance

---

### 5. Legal Background & Challenges

**SCJN Ruling on Original PANAUT (April 25, 2022):**
- The Supreme Court declared the original PANAUT (which required biometric data) **unconstitutional**
- Found it disproportionate, an arbitrary interference with privacy, and creating unacceptable data breach risks
- The PANAUT never became operational

**Current scheme's legal design:**
- Based on different legislation (new Ley en Materia de Telecomunicaciones)
- Deliberately avoids biometric data to sidestep the SCJN's constitutional objections
- Some citizens are reportedly seeking **amparos** (injunctions) against the requirement
- Some users exploring foreign eSIMs as workarounds
- President Sheinbaum has publicly backed the registration, stating it does not invade privacy and is essential for investigations, particularly disappearance cases

**Cost**: Infrastructure estimated at **4,053 million pesos (~$220.7 million USD)**, initially borne by telecom companies.

---

### 6. Key Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Apr 2021 | Senate approves PANAUT (with biometrics) |
| Apr 2022 | SCJN declares PANAUT unconstitutional |
| 2025 | Constitutional reform creates CRT; new non-biometric registration mandate enacted |
| Oct 2025 | CRT begins operations under Norma Solano |
| Jan 9, 2026 | Registration officially begins |
| Apr 21, 2026 | CRT confirms no prórroga; launches national campaign |
| May 27, 2026 | 51.6M lines registered (~33%) |
| May 28, 2026 | Castañeda publicly reaffirms: "no hay discusión alguna" |
| **Jun 30, 2026** | **DEADLINE** |
| Jul 1, 2026 | Unregistered lines face suspension |

---

key_facts: Mexico's mandatory cell phone line registration deadline is June 30, 2026 — the CRT has categorically confirmed NO prórroga (extension) will be granted.
Ricardo Castañeda Álvarez, CRT Director General de Política Regulatoria, stated on May 28, 2026: 'No hay discusión alguna' about an extension, noting only Congress can modify the deadline.
As of May 27, 2026, only ~51.6 million of ~144–158 million active lines have been registered (~33%), making full compliance by the deadline extremely unlikely.
Unregistered lines will be suspended (not canceled) starting July 1, 2026 — they can still call 911 but cannot make calls, send SMS, or use data.
Users can reactivate suspended lines at any time by completing registration without losing their number or balance.
The current registration requires only CURP and official ID — NO biometric data — distinguishing it from the original PANAUT struck down by the SCJN in April 2022.
The CRT (Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones), led by Comisionada Presidenta Norma Solano, replaced the IFT in October 2025 as Mexico's telecom regulator.
Telecom industry formally requested an extension in January 2026 citing technical problems and costs ($3.45 pesos + IVA per registration attempt), but the CRT rejected the request.
Registration progress by carrier (mid-May 2026): AT&T ~29%, Bait ~28%, Telcel ~19%, Movistar ~16%.
Some citizens are seeking amparos (injunctions) or exploring foreign eSIMs to avoid the registration requirement.
Total infrastructure cost of the registration system estimated at 4,053 million pesos (~$220.7 million USD).
President Claudia Sheinbaum has publicly backed the registration, calling it essential for investigations especially in disappearance cases.
